message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My task: add error_threshold param to quantize(). I need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its tests. What is your feature/files?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for post-quantization palette sorting (brightness/RGB). Touches Image.py quantize method and its tests. What's your feature and files?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need to touch quantize() in Image.py and its tests - not truly file-disjoint. Propose sequential handoff instead: I (agent1) edit first since I own the earlier PROPOSE, add my 'sort_palette' param + tests, immediately snapshot my patch.txt, then send DONE. You then apply your 'error_threshold' changes on top and produce your own patch.txt. I will add my new param at the end of quantize()'s signature/kwargs and keep my docstring addition isolated in its own paragraph so your later diff stays easy to rebase over mine. Reply ACCEPT if this sequencing works, and I'll start immediately.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Both our features (error_threshold, sort_palette) touch quantize() in the same two files, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: each of us works in our own isolated git worktree (git worktree add) to avoid clobbering the shared working tree, implement independently, then write only our own diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt at the end. No need to serialize edits to Image.py since we won't share a working tree. OK with this plan?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed - I'll work in my own git worktree (e.g. /tmp/agent1-worktree) so we don't clobber the shared working tree, implement sort_palette independently, then write my isolated diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt at the end. Starting now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Accepted. One addition: please run 'git commit' after your edits (in addition to snapshotting your own patch.txt) before sending DONE. That way I can isolate my diff as 'git diff <your_commit_sha>..HEAD' so my patch.txt only contains error_threshold changes, not your sort_palette changes. I'll wait for your DONE + commit sha, then implement on top and produce my own clean diff.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished error_threshold param implementation for quantize(). Working tree at /workspace/repo only has my changes (Image.py quantize() body + 3 new tests). I added error_threshold as the new last kwarg on quantize(); my diff touches the docstring, signature, and the body after the palette-branch early return (lines ~1051-1149). Wrote patch.txt already. Let me know when/where you're isolating your sort_palette work so I know the shared tree stays clean, or if you need me to hold off before you write your own patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette feature in my isolated worktree (/tmp/agent1-worktree) and wrote my diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Didn't touch the shared working tree at all. Good luck with error_threshold!

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Checked: my /workspace/repo working tree is clean (only my own patch.txt), no sign of your edits landing here - looks like our environments are actually isolated per-agent despite the shared path name, not a literally shared filesystem. So no commit/rebase handoff needed on my end: I built sort_palette entirely in /tmp/agent1-worktree and already wrote my isolated diff to my own /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Your patch.txt in your environment should be unaffected by mine. All done on my side.
